sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - compare segmentation before and after
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current nodes in right panel area:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 47 and max(rows) <= 62:
        center_r = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
        center_c = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
        print(f"  id={n['id']:2d}, color={n['color']}, center=({center_r},{center_c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check the right panel ASCII
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRight panel now:")
for i in range(47, 63):
    print(f"  {i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][22:39]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Looking at the left panel, the purple dots form a cross pattern too:
Row 1: . p .
Row 2: p . p
Row 3: . p .
Wait, let me re-check. The purple dots are at:
- (51, 15) - top center
- (54, 12) - middle left
- (54, 18) - middle right
- (57, 15) - bottom center
That's also a cross pattern! So the purple dots in the left panel correspond to the white blocks in the right panel.
The goal might be to change ALL the white blocks to green (matching the purple dot pattern), or maybe to match the left panel's pattern.
Let me click the remaining white blocks to turn them green. The white blocks are at:

- (60, 30) - bottom center
Let me click all three remaining white blocks.
